metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from Maria's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. Maria swept the floor carefully.

The chef washed the ripe tomatoes under cold running water. The chef sliced the ripe tomatoes into thin rounds on the board. The chef arranged the ripe tomatoes on the white serving plate. The chef drizzled olive oil and salt over the ripe tomatoes.

The dog discovered the large bone buried in the backyard soil. The dog carried the large bone to the front porch of the house. The dog chewed on the large bone for the entire afternoon. The dog buried the large bone under the old oak tree.

The painter stirred the blue paint inside the metal bucket carefully. The painter dipped the wide brush into the blue paint. The painter applied the blue paint to the bedroom wall in smooth strokes. The bedroom wall dried to a bright and even blue finish.

The student opened the heavy textbook to the first chapter. The student read the first chapter of the heavy textbook twice. The student wrote detailed notes about the first chapter on lined paper. The student passed the exam on the first chapter with high marks.

The gardener dug a deep hole beside the wooden fence in spring. The gardener placed the young rose bush into the deep hole gently. The gardener filled the deep hole with dark rich soil carefully. The young rose bush grew tall beside the wooden fence by summer.

The baker measured three cups of white flour into the mixing bowl. The baker added warm water and yeast to the white flour. The baker kneaded the dough on the floured wooden counter for ten minutes. The dough rose to double the original size inside the mixing bowl.

The carpenter selected a long oak plank from the lumber pile. The carpenter cut the long oak plank to the correct length. The carpenter sanded the long oak plank until the surface felt smooth. The carpenter attached the long oak plank to the wall as a shelf.

The old cat climbed the tall maple tree in the front yard. The old cat reached the highest branch of the tall maple tree. The old cat became stuck on the highest branch and meowed loudly. The firefighter rescued the old cat from the tall maple tree safely.

The mechanic raised the red truck on the hydraulic lift in the garage. The mechanic removed the worn brake pads from the red truck carefully. The mechanic installed new brake pads on the red truck in thirty minutes. The mechanic lowered the red truck back down to the garage floor.

Dark clouds gathered over the valley early in the morning hours. Heavy rain poured down on the valley for several long hours. The river running through the valley rose well above the muddy banks. The valley farmers moved the livestock to the dry high ground.

The hiker noticed storm clouds forming above the mountain trail ahead. The hiker packed the gear into the large green backpack quickly. The hiker descended the mountain trail at a fast and steady pace. The hiker reached the cabin at the base before the storm arrived.

The strong wind knocked the old fence down in the backyard. The old fence landed on the flower bed and crushed several plants. The homeowner cleared the broken pieces of the old fence away by evening. The homeowner built a new sturdy fence around the backyard the following week.

The sun heated the dry forest floor throughout the long summer afternoon. A small spark ignited the dry leaves on the forest floor. The fire spread quickly across the forest floor toward the creek. The forest ranger called for a water helicopter to fight the fire.

The temperature dropped below freezing during the cold winter night sharply. The water inside the exposed garden pipe froze solid by dawn. The frozen water expanded and cracked the exposed garden pipe open. The plumber replaced the cracked section of the exposed garden pipe that morning.

The earthquake shook the coastal town for nearly forty five seconds. Several old brick buildings in the coastal town crumbled to the ground. Dust and debris filled the narrow streets of the coastal town immediately. Rescue workers arrived at the coastal town within the first hour.

Lightning struck the tall pine tree on the hill during the storm. The tall pine tree on the hill split down the middle completely. The split trunk of the tall pine tree fell across the dirt road. A road crew removed the fallen trunk from the dirt road by noon.

The snow fell heavily on the mountain pass throughout the entire night. The mountain pass became blocked by deep snowdrifts before dawn arrived. The plow truck cleared the snowdrifts from the mountain pass slowly. Traffic began moving through the mountain pass again by late morning.

The volcano released a thick plume of ash into the clear sky. The thick ash plume drifted over the nearby farming village by afternoon. A gray layer of volcanic ash covered the rooftops of the farming village. The farming village residents cleared the volcanic ash from the rooftops carefully.

The river flooded the low meadow after three straight days of rain. The floodwater in the low meadow destroyed the wooden footbridge completely. A construction crew built a temporary crossing over the flooded low meadow. The floodwater in the low meadow receded after one full week of sunshine.

The cook cracked six brown eggs into the large ceramic mixing bowl. The cook whisked the brown eggs with a fork until smooth and frothy. The cook poured the whisked eggs into the hot cast iron skillet. The eggs in the hot cast iron skillet cooked in under two minutes.

The fisherman cast the long fishing line into the calm lake at sunrise. A large trout grabbed the bait on the long fishing line underwater. The fisherman reeled the large trout in with steady careful turns of the handle. The fisherman placed the large trout into the cooler on the dock.

The potter shaped the wet clay into a tall vase on the wheel. The potter placed the tall clay vase into the hot kiln overnight. The potter removed the hardened tall vase from the hot kiln in the morning. The potter painted a blue floral pattern on the hardened tall vase carefully.

The beekeeper opened the wooden hive in the corner of the orchard. The beekeeper lifted the heavy frame full of golden honeycomb from the hive. The beekeeper carried the heavy frame of honeycomb to the extraction shed. The beekeeper spun the golden honey out of the frame into glass jars.

The tailor measured the customer's shoulders with a yellow cloth tape carefully. The tailor cut the dark gray fabric according to the recorded measurements. The tailor sewed the pieces of dark gray fabric together on the machine. The customer tried on the finished dark gray suit coat with satisfaction.

The blacksmith heated the iron bar in the forge until the bar glowed red. The blacksmith hammered the glowing iron bar on the heavy steel anvil repeatedly. The blacksmith shaped the iron bar into a long curved horseshoe on the anvil. The blacksmith cooled the finished horseshoe in a bucket of cold water.

The librarian noticed water dripping from the ceiling onto the reference shelf. The librarian moved the books from the reference shelf to a dry table. The librarian covered the reference shelf with a large plastic sheet quickly. The maintenance worker repaired the leaking ceiling pipe above the reference shelf.

The pilot noticed a warning light flashing on the cockpit instrument panel. The pilot reported the warning light to the control tower by radio immediately. The control tower cleared the runway for the pilot to land right away. The pilot landed the plane on the cleared runway without any problems.

The photographer set up the tripod on the rocky cliff above the ocean. The photographer adjusted the camera lens to capture the setting sun perfectly. The photographer pressed the shutter button at the exact moment of the sunset. The photograph of the sunset over the ocean won the annual contest.

The weaver stretched the cotton threads across the wooden loom frame tightly. The weaver passed the shuttle through the cotton threads back and forth carefully. The weaver pressed each new row of thread down with the wooden beater. The weaver completed the colorful blanket on the wooden loom after three days.

The dentist examined the patient's teeth with a small angled mirror carefully. The dentist found a cavity in the patient's lower left molar during the exam. The dentist drilled the cavity out of the patient's lower left molar gently. The dentist filled the patient's lower left molar with a white composite material.

The locksmith examined the jammed front door lock with a small flashlight. The locksmith inserted a thin pick tool into the jammed front door lock. The locksmith turned the pick tool inside the jammed lock with a quick motion. The jammed front door lock clicked open after the locksmith's careful work.

The sculptor carved the block of white marble with a sharp steel chisel. The sculptor shaped the block of white marble into a seated human figure. The sculptor polished the surface of the seated figure until the marble shone brightly. The finished seated figure stood in the gallery for the spring exhibition.

The farmer plowed the wide wheat field with the old red tractor early. The farmer scattered wheat seeds across the plowed field by hand in the afternoon. Spring rain soaked the plowed wheat field for several weeks in a row. Tall green wheat stalks covered the wide field by the middle of summer.

The nurse checked the patient's blood pressure with the cuff on the left arm. The nurse recorded the patient's blood pressure reading on the medical chart carefully. The nurse noticed the patient's blood pressure reading was higher than normal range. The nurse informed the doctor about the patient's elevated blood pressure reading immediately.

The jeweler placed the rough diamond under the magnifying lens on the workbench. The jeweler studied the rough diamond and marked the cutting lines with ink. The jeweler cut the rough diamond along the marked lines with a precision saw. The jeweler polished the cut diamond until every facet sparkled under the bright lamp.

The conductor raised the baton in front of the full orchestra on stage. The full orchestra on stage began the opening passage of the symphony together. The full orchestra played the symphony with power and precision for ninety minutes. The audience in the concert hall rose and applauded the full orchestra warmly.

The detective examined the muddy footprints on the kitchen floor of the cabin. The detective photographed the muddy footprints on the kitchen floor from several angles. The detective compared the muddy footprints to the suspect's boot size and tread pattern. The muddy footprints on the kitchen floor matched the suspect's boots exactly.

The bricklayer spread wet mortar along the top of the garden wall evenly. The bricklayer pressed a red brick firmly into the wet mortar on the wall. The bricklayer checked the level of the red brick with a small spirit level. The bricklayer repeated the process until the garden wall reached the planned height.

The astronomer pointed the large telescope toward the eastern part of the night sky. The astronomer adjusted the focus dial on the large telescope very slowly and carefully. The astronomer spotted a faint comet through the large telescope near the constellation Orion. The astronomer recorded the position of the faint comet in the observation log.

The child built a tall sandcastle near the water's edge on the beach. The child decorated the tall sandcastle with small white shells and smooth pebbles. A large wave rolled up the beach toward the tall sandcastle at high tide. The large wave washed the tall sandcastle away and flattened the sand completely.

The electrician turned off the power to the kitchen at the main breaker. The electrician removed the old light fixture from the kitchen ceiling carefully. The electrician connected the new light fixture to the wires in the kitchen ceiling. The electrician turned the power back on and the new light fixture worked perfectly.

The train pulled into the small rural station ten minutes behind the normal schedule. The passengers on the train gathered bags and coats before the doors opened fully. The passengers stepped off the train onto the narrow platform of the small station. The train pulled away from the small rural station and continued down the track.

The boy threw a flat stone across the surface of the calm pond. The flat stone skipped four times across the surface of the calm pond. The flat stone created small circular ripples on the calm pond with each skip. The flat stone sank beneath the surface of the calm pond after the fourth skip.

The mail carrier placed a large brown package on the front porch of the house. The homeowner found the large brown package on the front porch after work. The homeowner opened the large brown package on the kitchen table with scissors. The homeowner discovered a set of new gardening tools inside the large brown package.

The lifeguard noticed a swimmer struggling in the deep end of the public pool. The lifeguard blew the whistle and jumped into the deep end of the public pool. The lifeguard reached the struggling swimmer in the deep end within seconds. The lifeguard pulled the struggling swimmer safely to the edge of the public pool.

The watchmaker removed the tiny gears from the antique pocket watch with tweezers. The watchmaker cleaned the tiny gears of the antique pocket watch in a solvent bath. The watchmaker placed the cleaned tiny gears back into the antique pocket watch carefully. The antique pocket watch ticked again perfectly after the watchmaker finished the repair.

The window washer climbed the tall ladder to the second floor of the office building. The window washer sprayed soapy water on the second floor office windows generously. The window washer wiped the soapy office windows clean with a rubber squeegee blade. The second floor office windows sparkled in the afternoon sunlight after the cleaning.

The ranger spotted fresh bear tracks on the muddy trail near the campground. The ranger followed the fresh bear tracks along the muddy trail for two miles. The ranger found the bear eating berries in a clearing at the end of the trail. The ranger observed the bear from a safe distance and recorded the sighting.

The piano tuner opened the lid of the grand piano in the concert hall. The piano tuner struck each key of the grand piano and listened for pitch problems. The piano tuner adjusted the tension of several strings inside the grand piano carefully. The grand piano in the concert hall sounded perfectly in tune after the adjustment.

The archeologist brushed sand away from the stone tablet at the dig site. The archeologist uncovered carved symbols on the surface of the stone tablet slowly. The archeologist photographed the carved symbols on the stone tablet from several angles. The archeologist wrapped the stone tablet in padded cloth and placed the tablet in a crate.

The baker rolled the bread dough into a long shape on the floured counter. The baker placed the shaped bread dough onto the oiled metal baking sheet carefully. The baker slid the metal baking sheet into the hot oven at the right temperature. The bread on the metal baking sheet rose and turned golden brown in the oven.

The chimney sweep climbed onto the roof of the old brick house carefully. The chimney sweep lowered the long brush down through the chimney of the old brick house. The chimney sweep scraped thick soot from the inside walls of the chimney. The chimney of the old brick house drew clean air after the thorough sweeping.

The surgeon washed both hands with antiseptic soap at the scrub station carefully. The surgeon made a small incision on the patient's right knee with a scalpel. The surgeon repaired the torn ligament inside the patient's right knee with precision instruments. The surgeon closed the incision on the patient's right knee with fine stitches.

The zookeeper prepared a large bucket of fresh fruit for the elephant enclosure. The zookeeper carried the large bucket of fresh fruit across the zoo grounds. The zookeeper placed the fresh fruit inside the feeding trough of the elephant enclosure. The elephants in the enclosure walked to the feeding trough and ate the fresh fruit.

The postal clerk weighed the small cardboard box on the counter scale carefully. The postal clerk applied the correct amount of postage stamps to the small cardboard box. The postal clerk placed the small cardboard box in the outgoing mail bin at the back. The small cardboard box traveled by truck to the regional sorting facility overnight.

The glassblower gathered molten glass on the end of the long iron blowpipe. The glassblower blew air through the long iron blowpipe to form a hollow sphere. The glassblower shaped the hollow glass sphere into a delicate vase with metal tools. The glassblower placed the delicate glass vase in the cooling oven for the night.

The crossing guard stepped into the busy intersection and raised the stop sign high. The cars on both sides of the busy intersection came to a complete stop. The group of school children crossed the busy intersection safely in a single line. The crossing guard stepped back onto the curb and lowered the stop sign.

The typist inserted a blank sheet of paper into the old manual typewriter carefully. The typist pressed the keys of the old manual typewriter with quick steady fingers. The typist filled the entire sheet of paper with neat rows of black text. The typist pulled the finished sheet of paper out of the old manual typewriter.

The plumber shut off the water valve under the kitchen sink of the apartment. The plumber removed the clogged drain pipe from under the kitchen sink with a wrench. The plumber cleared the grease blockage from the clogged drain pipe with a snake tool. The plumber reconnected the clean drain pipe under the kitchen sink and opened the valve.

The barber draped a cloth cape over the customer sitting in the leather barber chair. The barber trimmed the customer's hair with sharp scissors in quick even strokes. The barber shaved the back of the customer's neck with a straight razor carefully. The barber removed the cloth cape and brushed loose hair from the customer's shoulders.

The falconer released the trained hawk from the thick leather glove into the open sky. The trained hawk soared high above the open meadow and circled in wide arcs. The trained hawk dove toward a rabbit running across the open meadow at great speed. The falconer called the trained hawk back to the thick leather glove with a whistle.

The night guard heard a loud crash from the storage room of the warehouse. The night guard walked to the storage room and unlocked the heavy metal door. The night guard discovered a broken window and scattered boxes inside the dark storage room. The night guard reported the break-in at the warehouse to the local police immediately.

The boat captain steered the fishing vessel out of the harbor at first light. The boat captain navigated the fishing vessel past the rocky point into open water. The crew on the fishing vessel cast the wide nets into the deep ocean waters. The crew hauled the wide nets full of silver fish back onto the fishing vessel.

The cobbler examined the worn sole of the brown leather boot on the workbench. The cobbler cut a new sole from a thick piece of leather with sharp shears. The cobbler glued and stitched the new sole onto the brown leather boot firmly. The brown leather boot with the new sole looked and felt like a new pair.

The chemist poured the blue liquid into the glass beaker on the lab counter. The chemist heated the glass beaker of blue liquid over the small gas burner slowly. The blue liquid in the glass beaker changed color from blue to bright green quickly. The chemist recorded the color change of the liquid in the experiment notebook.

The janitor noticed a large water stain on the ceiling of the school hallway. The janitor placed a yellow bucket on the floor under the water stain to catch drips. The janitor climbed the stepladder and inspected the source of the leak above the ceiling. The janitor sealed the cracked pipe above the ceiling with waterproof tape temporarily.

The deep sea diver descended into the dark water above the old shipwreck slowly. The deep sea diver reached the wooden hull of the old shipwreck on the ocean floor. The deep sea diver found a barnacle-covered chest inside the cabin of the old shipwreck. The deep sea diver brought the barnacle-covered chest to the surface for examination.

The taxi driver picked up the passenger at the corner of Main and Oak streets. The taxi driver drove the passenger through the crowded downtown streets toward the airport. The taxi driver arrived at the airport terminal with the passenger in twenty five minutes. The passenger paid the taxi driver the fare and walked into the airport terminal.

The birdwatcher set up the spotting scope at the edge of the wetland marsh. The birdwatcher scanned the surface of the wetland marsh through the spotting scope carefully. The birdwatcher spotted a rare blue heron standing in the shallow marsh water. The birdwatcher recorded the rare blue heron sighting in the field notebook with the date.

The roofer climbed the extension ladder to the damaged section of the house roof. The roofer pried the cracked shingles off the damaged section of the house roof. The roofer nailed new shingles into place on the damaged section of the house roof. The roofer climbed down the extension ladder after the house roof repair was complete.

The oyster farmer lowered the wire cages into the cold bay water from the dock. The oyster farmer waited eight months for the oysters in the wire cages to grow. The oyster farmer pulled the wire cages out of the cold bay water by crane. The oyster farmer sorted the mature oysters from the wire cages by size on the dock.

The translator read the original French letter from the archive folder very carefully. The translator wrote an English version of the French letter on a separate sheet. The translator compared the English version to the original French letter for accuracy twice. The translator delivered the finished English version of the French letter to the client.

The surveyor set up the tripod and theodolite at the corner of the empty lot. The surveyor measured the angles and distances of the empty lot with the theodolite. The surveyor marked the boundary lines of the empty lot with wooden stakes and string. The surveyor drew an accurate map of the empty lot based on the measurements.

The clockmaker removed the pendulum from the old grandfather clock in the foyer. The clockmaker cleaned each brass gear of the old grandfather clock with a soft cloth. The clockmaker oiled the moving parts of the old grandfather clock with precision drops. The old grandfather clock in the foyer kept perfect time after the clockmaker's careful repair.

The veterinarian examined the injured wing of the barn owl on the metal table. The veterinarian set the fractured bone in the injured wing with a tiny splint. The veterinarian wrapped the injured wing of the barn owl in soft white bandages gently. The barn owl with the bandaged wing rested in the recovery cage for six weeks.

The cartographer spread the old yellowed map across the wide table in the study. The cartographer compared the old yellowed map to modern satellite images on the computer screen. The cartographer marked the differences between the old map and modern images with red ink. The cartographer published a report about the geographic changes found on the old map.

The candlemaker melted blocks of white beeswax in the large copper pot on the stove. The candlemaker dipped long cotton wicks into the melted beeswax in the copper pot repeatedly. The layers of beeswax on the cotton wicks thickened with each careful dip. The candlemaker hung the finished beeswax candles on the drying rack to cool and harden.

The orchard worker picked ripe red apples from the tall apple trees all morning long. The orchard worker loaded the ripe red apples into large wooden crates on the trailer. The orchard worker drove the trailer of apple crates to the packing shed by noon. The packing shed workers sorted the ripe red apples by size and quality carefully.

The stone mason chiseled a square block from the rough granite slab with heavy tools. The stone mason smoothed the surfaces of the square granite block with a grinding wheel. The stone mason set the square granite block into the garden wall with mortar. The stone mason checked the alignment of the square granite block with a level.

The old woman knitted the first row of the green wool scarf on wooden needles. The old woman added row after row to the green wool scarf every evening for weeks. The old woman finished the green wool scarf and tied off the last stitch neatly. The old woman wrapped the green wool scarf in tissue paper as a birthday gift.

The tree surgeon examined the diseased branch of the elm tree in the park. The tree surgeon climbed the elm tree in the park with rope and harness gear. The tree surgeon sawed the diseased branch off the elm tree with a chainsaw carefully. The diseased branch of the elm tree fell safely to the ground in the park.

The upholsterer removed the old faded fabric from the antique armchair in the workshop. The upholsterer replaced the worn padding under the old fabric of the antique armchair. The upholsterer stretched new dark red fabric over the frame of the antique armchair tightly. The antique armchair in the workshop looked elegant and new after the upholsterer's careful work.

The harbor master spotted the cargo ship approaching the port through the morning fog slowly. The harbor master radioed docking instructions to the captain of the cargo ship immediately. The tugboat guided the cargo ship into the assigned berth at the port carefully. The dock workers tied the cargo ship to the iron bollards with thick heavy ropes.

The tiler spread thin-set adhesive on the bathroom floor with a notched metal trowel. The tiler pressed each white ceramic tile into the adhesive on the bathroom floor firmly. The tiler checked the spacing between the white ceramic tiles with small plastic spacers. The tiler filled the gaps between the white ceramic tiles with gray grout the following day.

The diver noticed a crack in the underwater pipeline during the routine morning inspection. The diver marked the location of the crack on the underwater pipeline with bright tape. The diver surfaced and reported the crack in the underwater pipeline to the supervisor on deck. The repair team sent equipment down to fix the crack in the underwater pipeline immediately.

The pastry chef sifted powdered sugar through a fine mesh sieve into the cream bowl. The pastry chef whipped the cream and powdered sugar together until soft peaks formed perfectly. The pastry chef spread the whipped cream over the top of the layered chocolate cake. The pastry chef placed the finished chocolate cake in the glass display case for customers.

The printer loaded thick white card stock into the tray of the large printing press. The printer aligned the metal type plates on the bed of the large printing press. The printer pulled the heavy lever of the large printing press down with both hands. The large printing press stamped clean black letters onto the thick white card stock perfectly.

The park ranger found a fallen tree blocking the main hiking trail after the windstorm. The park ranger measured the fallen tree and estimated the weight of the trunk and branches. The park ranger cut the fallen tree into sections with a chainsaw on the trail. The park ranger dragged the cut sections of the fallen tree off the main hiking trail.

The dressmaker pinned the paper pattern pieces onto the silk fabric on the cutting table. The dressmaker cut the silk fabric along the edges of the paper pattern pieces carefully. The dressmaker sewed the cut silk pieces together on the sewing machine stitch by stitch. The dressmaker hung the finished silk dress on the mannequin in the shop window.

The lighthouse keeper climbed the spiral stone staircase to the top of the old lighthouse. The lighthouse keeper cleaned the large glass lens at the top of the old lighthouse. The lighthouse keeper replaced the burned-out bulb behind the large glass lens with a new one. The bright beam from the old lighthouse shone across the dark ocean waters that night.

The geologist collected rock samples from the exposed cliff face along the river valley. The geologist carried the rock samples back to the field laboratory in a canvas bag. The geologist examined the rock samples under a microscope at the field laboratory bench. The geologist identified ancient marine fossils embedded in the rock samples from the cliff face.

The beeswax chandler heated the old wax seal stamp over the small candle flame carefully. The beeswax chandler pressed the hot wax seal stamp into a pool of red wax firmly. The red wax on the envelope cooled and hardened under the stamp in seconds. The beeswax chandler lifted the stamp to reveal a perfect crest impression in the red wax.

The cab driver stopped at the red traffic light on the corner of Elm Avenue. A pedestrian crossed the street in front of the cab at the red traffic light. The traffic light on the corner of Elm Avenue turned from red to green again. The cab driver pressed the gas pedal and continued down Elm Avenue past the intersection.

The mountain guide checked the rope harnesses of the climbing group at the base camp. The mountain guide led the climbing group up the steep rocky face of the north ridge. The climbing group reached the summit of the north ridge by early afternoon together. The mountain guide took a photograph of the climbing group standing on the summit.

The radio operator received a distress signal on the emergency frequency late at night. The radio operator traced the distress signal to a fishing boat off the northern coast. The radio operator relayed the location of the fishing boat to the coast guard station. The coast guard station dispatched a rescue vessel to the fishing boat within ten minutes.

The bookbinder removed the torn cover from the old leather-bound volume on the workbench. The bookbinder cut a new piece of brown leather to match the size of the old volume. The bookbinder glued the new brown leather cover onto the spine of the old volume. The bookbinder stamped the title in gold letters on the new brown leather cover.

The cider maker poured bushels of fresh apples into the large wooden press hopper. The cider maker turned the iron screw of the large wooden press down with effort. The juice from the crushed apples flowed out of the large wooden press into buckets. The cider maker poured the fresh apple juice from the buckets into oak barrels for storage.

The florist selected a dozen long-stemmed red roses from the cooler in the shop. The florist trimmed the stems of the red roses at an angle with sharp shears. The florist arranged the red roses with baby's breath in a clear glass vase carefully. The florist wrapped the clear glass vase in gold foil paper for the customer.

The sailor noticed dark water seeping through the hull of the wooden sailboat below deck. The sailor stuffed rags into the crack in the hull of the wooden sailboat quickly. The sailor pumped the rising water out of the wooden sailboat with the hand bilge pump. The sailor steered the wooden sailboat back to the marina for a proper hull repair.
noncausal_texts: A baker sold warm bread at the market. A fiddler played cheerful tunes near the fountain. Two children fed pigeons beside the well. A farmer weighed ripe apples on a scale.

A violinist performed a slow waltz in the candlelit dining room. A saxophonist played jazz standards at the rooftop bar downtown. A cellist practiced scales inside the small rehearsal studio all morning. A drummer kept a steady beat at the outdoor summer festival stage.

A retired teacher planted daffodils along the stone path in the garden. A young couple hung a bird feeder from the branch of the pine tree. A mail carrier noticed fresh tulips blooming beside the front porch of the corner house. An elderly neighbor watered the potted ferns on the apartment balcony at dusk.

A sculptor shaped wet clay into a horse figure at the community art center. A photographer captured the sunset over the lake from the wooden dock. A quilter stitched a star pattern onto the blue cotton blanket in the living room. A wood carver whittled a small bird from a block of cedar on the back porch.

A florist arranged sunflowers in a tall glass vase behind the shop counter. A beekeeper collected golden honey from the white hive boxes in the meadow. A fruit vendor stacked bright oranges into a pyramid on the market table. A chocolatier poured warm cocoa into small copper molds at the kitchen counter.

A geologist studied a layer of red sandstone on the desert canyon wall. A marine biologist tagged a sea turtle on the sandy beach at dawn. A botanist pressed wildflower samples between the pages of a heavy field notebook. An entomologist pinned a rare beetle specimen to the board inside the university lab.

A trumpet player performed a solo piece at the winter jazz concert downtown. A folk singer strummed an acoustic guitar on the porch of the country inn. A pianist played a soft melody in the lobby of the grand hotel all evening. A harmonica player entertained the crowd at the riverside picnic area on Saturday.

A deep sea fisherman hauled a net full of cod from the cold Atlantic waters. A rice farmer harvested golden grain from the flooded paddy field by hand. A sheep rancher sheared thick wool from the flock in the big red barn. A coffee grower dried fresh beans on wide mats under the tropical sun.

A cobbler repaired the worn heel of a brown leather boot at the downtown shop. A watchmaker replaced the tiny spring inside an antique pocket watch with steady hands. A locksmith cut a new brass key for the front door of the old townhouse. A seamstress mended a torn silk sleeve at the sewing table by the window.

A trail runner completed the forest loop in under forty five minutes before breakfast. A swimmer crossed the width of the cold mountain lake without stopping once that morning. A rock climber reached the top of the granite wall at the outdoor climbing park. A cyclist rode the steep coastal road from the harbor to the hilltop lighthouse.

A nurse administered a flu vaccine at the community health clinic on Tuesday. A dentist filled a cavity for a patient in the downtown dental office after lunch. A pharmacist counted pills behind the counter of the neighborhood drugstore that afternoon. A paramedic treated a sprained ankle at the soccer field near the elementary school.

A potter glazed a set of ceramic bowls in the backyard kiln over the weekend. A jeweler polished a silver ring on the felt wheel inside the small workshop. A glassblower formed a round ornament at the end of the iron pipe at the studio. A basket weaver braided long reeds into a wide tray at the craft fair booth.

A librarian shelved returned novels in the fiction aisle of the public library quietly. A museum guard stood beside the marble statue in the east wing of the gallery. A bookshop owner arranged a window display of new mystery titles for the holiday sale. An archivist filed old newspaper clippings into labeled folders in the basement storage room.

A plumber fixed a dripping faucet in the upstairs bathroom of the brick rowhouse. A roofer replaced cracked tiles on the south side of the old stone cottage. An electrician installed a ceiling fan in the master bedroom of the lakeside cabin. A glazier cut a new pane of glass for the broken kitchen window at the farmhouse.

A calligrapher wrote a poem on parchment paper with a gold-tipped pen at the desk. A printmaker pulled an ink proof from the carved wooden block at the press studio. A muralist painted a large sunflower on the east wall of the community center building. A mosaic artist pressed colored tiles into wet cement on the fountain at the park.

A ferry captain steered the boat across the foggy harbor before sunrise that cold morning. A bus driver picked up passengers at the station on the edge of town after dawn. A bicycle messenger delivered a sealed envelope to the law office on the fifth floor. A horse trainer guided a young mare through the obstacle course at the riding arena.

A cheesemaker aged a wheel of cheddar in the stone cellar for nine months straight. A winemaker crushed dark grapes in the wooden vat at the family vineyard in October. A jam maker boiled strawberries with sugar in the copper pot on the farmhouse stove. A pickle maker sealed jars of cucumbers with dill and vinegar in the kitchen pantry.

A meteorologist tracked a storm system moving across the Gulf on the radar screen. A seismologist recorded a mild tremor on the sensor array near the mountain base camp. A volcanologist collected gas samples from a vent on the slope of the active crater. An oceanographer measured water temperature at a depth station in the Pacific trench area.

A violin maker carved the maple back of a new instrument at the workbench in Milan. A drum maker stretched a goat hide over the wooden shell in the Accra workshop. A flute maker drilled tone holes in a length of grenadilla wood at the London studio. A guitar maker glued spruce bracing inside the body of an acoustic instrument in Nashville.

A wool dyer soaked raw fleece in a vat of indigo dye at the textile mill. A silk weaver passed bright threads through the hand loom at the artisan cooperative. A leather tanner scraped a cowhide on the wooden beam behind the workshop shed. A cotton spinner fed raw fiber into the old spinning wheel at the heritage museum.
